source_paper_title: Lower Complexity Bounds of First-Order Methods for Affinely Constrained Composite Nonconvex Problems
context:
Page 25, Section 4 (Conclusion and Open Questions)

"First, for Algorithm Class 1 on solving Problem Class 1, can the lower bound \(O(\kappa([A\u00af ;A])L_f\Delta F_0\,\u01eb^{-2})\) be achieved, i.e., is it tight?"
title: Tightness of the lower complexity bound for Algorithm Class 1
latex:
Let \(d,n,\bar n\in\mathbb{N}\). Consider the affinely constrained composite optimization problem
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^d}\ F_0(x):=f_0(x)+g(x)\quad\text{s.t.}\quad Ax+b=0,
\]
where:
- \(A\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times d\}\), \(b\in\mathbb{R}^n\).
- \(f_0:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\) is differentiable and \(L_f\)-smooth, i.e., \(\|\nabla f_0(x)-\nabla f_0(x')\|\le L_f\|x-x'\|\) for all \(x,x'\).
- \(g(x)=\bar g(\bar A x+\bar b)\) for some \(\bar A\in\mathbb{R}^{\bar n\times d\}\), \(\bar b\in\mathbb{R}^{\bar n}\), and a proper lower semicontinuous convex function \(\bar g:\mathbb{R}^{\bar n}\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) with \(\mathrm{relint}(\mathrm{dom}(\bar g))\ne\emptyset\) and \(\mathrm{dom}(\bar g)\) not a singleton.
Define the stacked matrix \([\bar A;A]\in\mathbb{R}^{(\bar n+n)\times d}\) and its (positive-eigenvalue) condition number
\[
\kappa([\bar A;A]) := \sqrt{\frac{\lambda_{\max}([\bar A;A][\bar A;A]^\top)}{\lambda^+_{\min}([\bar A;A][\bar A;A]^\top)}}.
\]
For \(\varepsilon>0\), call \(x\in\mathbb{R}^d\) an \(\varepsilon\)-stationary point if there exists \(\gamma\in\mathbb{R}^n\) such that
\[
\max\Big\{\mathrm{dist}\big(0,\nabla f_0(x)+A^\top\gamma+\partial g(x)\big),\ \|Ax+b\|\Big\}\le \varepsilon.
\]
Let \(\Delta F_0 := F_0(x^{(0)})-\inf_x F_0(x)\) for a given initialization \(x^{(0)}\).
An algorithm belongs to Algorithm Class 1 if it generates iterates \(\{x^{(t)}\}_{t\ge1}\) using only calls to the oracle
\(\mathrm{ORACLE1}(x,z,\eta)=(\nabla f_0(x),Ax,A^\top z,\mathrm{prox}_{\eta g}(x))\) and linear-span updates as defined in the paper.

Problem: Determine whether there exists a first-order method in Algorithm Class 1 and a constant \(C>0\) (possibly up to logarithmic factors) such that, for every instance in the above problem class and every \(\varepsilon\in(0,1)\), it outputs an \(\varepsilon\)-stationary point using at most
\[
C\,\kappa([\bar A;A])\,L_f\,\Delta F_0\,\varepsilon^{-2}
\]
oracle calls to \(\mathrm{ORACLE1}\) (or \(\tilde O(\kappa([\bar A;A])L_f\Delta F_0\varepsilon^{-2})\) allowing polylogarithmic factors). Equivalently, is the lower bound \(\Omega(\kappa([\bar A;A])L_f\Delta F_0\varepsilon^{-2})\) proved for Algorithm Class 1 tight?
open_problem_quote: "First, for Algorithm Class 1 on solving Problem Class 1, can the lower bound \(O(\kappa([A\u00af ;A])L_f\Delta F_0\,\u01eb^{-2})\) be achieved, i.e., is it tight?"

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
